title: First prenatal visit: which week to book
excerpt: When to schedule your first antenatal visit, common tests, and how to prepare — Medlatec, WHO.
content:
## Recommended timing

Many guidelines suggest **8–12 weeks** or as soon as you know you are pregnant—especially with medical conditions, medications, pain, or bleeding.

## What to expect

- History, medications, blood pressure.
- Blood and urine tests (varies by clinic).
- Ultrasound to confirm intrauterine pregnancy and dating.

## Bring

- Medication/vitamin list and questions about diet, nausea, activity.
- Prior lab results if available.

## Urgent signs

Bleeding or severe one-sided pain—seek care promptly (rule out ectopic pregnancy).
